message:
release: rename textsift-native-<triple> → textsift-<triple>

User-facing per-triple subpackage names drop the '-native-' infix:
'textsift-linux-x64', 'textsift-linux-arm64', 'textsift-darwin-x64',
'textsift-darwin-arm64', 'textsift-win32-x64'. Same six unscoped
packages; npm user 'teamch' owns them all. Single character per
package shorter, and the 'native' descriptor was redundant — the
umbrella is 'textsift', whatever's per-triple is by definition the
native binary.

Renames in: scripts/publish-natives.mjs, src/native/backend.ts (the
runtime resolver), packages/textsift/README.md, .github/workflows/
release.yml (artifact names + download pattern), docs/intro-source.md,
.gitignore.

The .node *file* inside each subpackage is still 'textsift-native.node'
— that's an internal implementation detail and doesn't affect the
public package name.

files.patch:
@@ -117,7 +117,7 @@ jobs:
       - name: Upload .node binary
         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
         with:
-          name: textsift-native-${{ matrix.triple }}
+          name: textsift-${{ matrix.triple }}
           path: packages/textsift/dist/textsift-native.node
           retention-days: 7
 
@@ -156,7 +156,7 @@ jobs:
         uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
         with:
           path: native-bins
-          pattern: textsift-native-*
+          pattern: textsift-*
 
       - run: npm install --no-audit --no-fund

@@ -30,6 +30,6 @@ packages/textsift/vendor/dawn/lib/*.dll
 # across OS by scripts/bench-aggregate.mjs into the workflow summary).
 bench-results.json
 # Per-triple subpackage staging dir written by scripts/publish-natives.mjs.
-# Contents are published to npm as textsift-native-{linux-x64,...} and
+# Contents are published to npm as textsift-{linux-x64,...} and
 # linked from the umbrella package's optionalDependencies.
 dist-pkg/

@@ -171,7 +171,7 @@ opts.backend === "auto"    → probe; WebGPU if shader-f16 available,
 ```
 
 The umbrella `textsift` package's resolver tries the per-platform
-native binding first via `require("textsift-native-${process.platform}-${process.arch}/textsift-native.node")`.
+native binding first via `require("textsift-${process.platform}-${process.arch}/textsift-native.node")`.
 If the .node fails to load (no GPU, no Vulkan loader, missing
 binary, etc.), the resolver falls through to the WASM backend.
 Throwing the failure is gated behind explicit `offline: true` mode
@@ -512,7 +512,7 @@ measurement.
 binary on each runner, uploads as artifact, then a single `publish`
 job pulls all artifacts and publishes:
 
-- `textsift-native-{linux-x64,linux-arm64,darwin-x64,darwin-arm64,
+- `textsift-{linux-x64,linux-arm64,darwin-x64,darwin-arm64,
   win32-x64}` (per-triple, single .node each)
 - `textsift` (umbrella, points at all 5 via `optionalDependencies`)

@@ -51,7 +51,7 @@ End-to-end on a 122-character input with 4 PII spans: `redact()` returns in ~50
 | Windows x86_64 | Dawn-direct | Tint → D3D12 via statically-linked Google Dawn |
 | (any platform, no GPU) | WASM fallback | Zig + SIMD128 in WebAssembly |
 
-Each platform's `.node` binary is built with comptime-gated Zig code so it only contains the relevant backend — Mac binaries don't ship Vulkan code, Windows binaries don't ship Obj-C, etc. npm picks the right `optionalDependencies` subpackage at install time (`textsift-native-{linux-x64,linux-arm64,darwin-x64,darwin-arm64,win32-x64}`).
+Each platform's `.node` binary is built with comptime-gated Zig code so it only contains the relevant backend — Mac binaries don't ship Vulkan code, Windows binaries don't ship Obj-C, etc. npm picks the right `optionalDependencies` subpackage at install time (`textsift-{linux-x64,linux-arm64,darwin-x64,darwin-arm64,win32-x64}`).
 
 ## Linux prereqs (one-time)
